Name: Counting Sort
Author: Harold H. Seward
Description: Counting sort is an integer sorting algorithm that operates by counting the number of objects that possess distinct key values, and applying prefix sum on those counts to determine the positions of each key value in the output sequence. Its running time is linear in the number of items and the difference between the maximum and minimum key values, so it is only suitable for direct use in situations where the variation in keys is not significantly greater than the number of items.
Algorithm: async function sort(){const r=array.length;let a=Math.max(...array);const t=new Array(a+1).fill(0);for(let a=0;a<r;a++)t[array[a]]++;for(let r=1;r<=a;r++)t[r]+=t[r-1];for(let a=r-1;a>=0;a--){if(swap(a,t[array[a]]-1),await sleep(),firstRun)return;t[array[a]]--}for(let a=0;a<r;a++)array[a]>array[a+1]&&await sort()}
